name: Bank overdraft fee
summary: A fee (typically $30-$38) charged when a checking account transaction exceeds the available balance and the bank pays the transaction anyway.

evidence.claim: CFPB 2024 Overdraft Lending Rule caps overdraft fees at $5 or the bank's actual cost for large banks ($10B+ in assets); challenged in court, effective date October 2025.
CFPB data: Americans paid $5.5B in overdraft and NSF fees in 2023, down from $12.6B in 2019 as banks dropped fees voluntarily (Capital One, Citi, Ally all to zero).
Regulation E (12 CFR §1005.17) requires banks to obtain opt-in consent before charging overdraft fees on ATM and one-time debit transactions.

body.description: When a checking-account transaction would overdraw the balance, the bank may: (a) decline it free (standard), (b) pay it and charge $30-$38 (courtesy pay / overdraft), or (c) transfer from savings/line of credit (may have separate fee). The highest-fee path is usually the default unless the customer changes settings.

identificationSignals.description: Reg E opt-in status determines whether fees can be charged on ATM/debit transactions (E3).
Multiple overdraft fees within 24h = extended overdraft pattern; flag for escalation.

disclosureLegality.requirement: CFPB 2024 Overdraft Lending Rule caps fees at $5 or actual cost for banks $10B+ in assets (E1), pending litigation.
Reg E (12 CFR §1005.17) requires opt-in consent for overdraft on ATM/one-time debit (E3). Without opt-in, the bank must decline the transaction free.

negotiability.script: Call your bank and ask: 'I noticed a $35 overdraft fee on my account. This is the first time (or once in 12 months). Can you please waive it as a courtesy?' Most major banks waive 1-2 fees per year on request — Chase, Wells Fargo, Bank of America all have documented goodwill-credit policies. If denied, ask for a supervisor and mention you are considering closing the account. Success rate is ~75% on first request. Also opt OUT of overdraft service under Reg E so future debit/ATM transactions simply decline instead of overdrawing.
